title: INDEC Argentina — CPI (post-Dec-2016 rebuild, official), National Accounts (GDP/supply-demand), Poverty & Indigence
source_org: Instituto Nacional de Estadística y Censos (INDEC), Argentina
time_coverage: CPI: national series by regional breakdown (aperturas) and division/category (divisiones), monthly, base December 2016 = 100 — this is the CREDIBLE POST-INDEC-SCANDAL series, i.e. post-rebuild after the 2007-2015 data-falsification period; National accounts (sh_oferta_demanda): quarterly supply/demand aggregates (GDP by expenditure) through Q3 2024 vintage file; income/national savings (sh_ingreso_ahorro_nac): through Aug 2025 vintage; Poverty/indigence (sh_pobrezaeindigencia_continua): semester series from EPH (Encuesta Permanente de Hogares), 31 urban agglomerates.
frequency: CPI monthly; national accounts quarterly; poverty semi-annual
units: CPI: index numbers (base Dec-2016=100) and % variation, described in file; GDP: current and constant pesos; poverty: % of population/households below poverty/indigence line
license_terms: INDEC public statistics (Argentine government open data)
notes: IMPORTANT PROVENANCE NOTE per project methodology: this is the POST-DECEMBER-2016-REBUILD CPI series, launched after INDEC's 2007-2015 statistics-falsification scandal (formal IMF censure 2013) discredited the prior official CPI. This post-2016 series is treated as credible/official by IMF and market participants; it should NOT be spliced backward through 2007-2015 without the Cavallo-Bertolotto reconstruction (see cavallo-bertolotto-inflation-1943-2016 dataset) or private trackers (e.g. PriceStats/Billion Prices Project), which independently estimated inflation during the blackout years because the pre-2016 official numbers from that window are not reliable. Files retrieved directly from INDEC's public FTP cuadros directory (found via search since the BasesDeDatos landing page itself is menu-only / JS-driven with no crawlable direct links). GDP file sh_oferta_demanda is the quarterly national-accounts supply/demand table (closest direct-download proxy for GDP by expenditure); a longer annual PIB time-back-series was not found as a single direct file in this session.
